author: @agm5424
text:
This happens when someone has spent way too long holding a set of ideas and believing them to be true without enough proper research to verify if they are actually true or not. The same goes for the ideas that they view as opposite to their own. They don't actually know or have a 20/20 understanding of the ideas that are not their own which is why they simply project their own negative traits on those that hold said ideas. 

This gets to the point of basing their identities on said beliefs to such an extreme extent that their minds, specifically the r-complex/ego, becomes alert and fearful of said ideas being proven wrong. This is because the r-complex is the part of the brain that focuses on physical safety and the safety of the mental ego, therefore when it encounters ideas that go against long held beliefs it reacts in the same way it would react as if encountering a threat to their physical safety. 

This leads to the person with this mindset to react to ideas that are different to theirs (and those who hold them) as if they are being attacked or in danger, to the point that they view the person speaking ideas opposite to them as a danger to their lives, which could lead to them reacting with hostility towards that people. So instead of either proving their ideas right or disproving the ideas opposite to theirs as wrong they simply try to paint those with said opposing ideas as if they are unreasonable people or they outright try to dehumanize them in order to silence them.

On the other hand an individual with a healthy mindset is not only capable of processing information without letting their emotions control them and becoming hostile but they actively seek and relish the opportunity of debating and putting said information to the test because they would be mature enough to understand that they are not infallible and can be wrong on what they believe to be true.
